task:
Build this app in Clear:

**Lead Router** (Level 7)

Build a lead routing API. Prospects submit leads (name, email, company, size). The server applies routing rules: SMB → alice, Mid-market → bob, Enterprise → charlie — set assigned_to automatically before saving. Need: POST /api/leads (submit + auto-route), GET /api/leads (list), GET /api/leads/new (only status='new').

Starting skeleton:
```clear
build for web and javascript backend

create a Leads table:
  name, required
  email, required
  s
final_source:
build for web and javascript backend
database is local memory

owner is 'admin@example.com'
allow signup and login

create a Leads table:
  name, required
  email, required
  company
  size, default 'SMB'
  assigned_to
  status, default 'new'

define function get_assigned_person(company_size):
  if company_size is 'Enterprise':
    send back 'charlie'
  else if company_size is 'Mid-market':
    send back 'bob'
  else:
    send back 'alice'

when user sends lead to /api/leads:
  validate lead:
    name is text, required
    email is text, required
  
  assigned_person = get_assigned_person(lead's size)
  lead's assigned_to is assigned_person
  
  saved = save lead to Leads
  send back saved with status 201

when user calls GET /api/leads:
  leads = get all Leads
  send back leads

when user calls GET /api/leads/new:
  new_leads = get all Leads where status is 'new'
  send back new_leads

page 'App' at '/':
  on page load get leads from '/api/leads'
  
  section 'Submit New Lead':
    lead_name = ''
    name = ''
    email = ''
    company = ''
    size = 'SMB'
    'Name' as text input saved as lead_name
    'Name' as text input saved as name
    'Email' as text input saved as email
    'Company' as text input saved as company
    'Size' as dropdown with ['SMB', 'Mid-market', 'Enterprise'] saved as size
    button 'Submit':
      send {name: lead_name, email: lead_email, company: lead_company, size: lead_size} as a new lead to '/api/leads'
      send {name, email, company, size} as a new lead to '/api/leads'
  
  section 'All Leads':
    display leads as table
